history.content:
<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. The open safe path is to my right, so I should turn right to face it and continue navigating toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward. The cell in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The goal is visible to my upper-left, but the safest immediate move is to turn left so I can head toward the open corridor on my left side and avoid the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The view matches the earlier situation: I am facing upward, and the cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. Turning right will orient me toward the safe open cell beside me, which is the next step toward navigating around the lava toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward at row 07 col D. The cell ahead is a wall, so I cannot go forward. To reach the open path in the middle and eventually the goal at B05, I should turn left to face the safe open cell at row 07 col C or the corridor leading upward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m back in the same local layout with the goal visible ahead-right and a lava cell directly in front. Forward is unsafe, so I should turn right to face the open safe cell and continue around the obstacle.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing up at row 07, column D. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. Turning left would face the open cell at row 07, column C, which is safe and keeps me in the corridor toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is still visible to the upper right. From my current position, the cell ahead is lava, so I should not move forward. The safest immediate step is to turn right to face the open path and continue around the obstacle.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07, column D, facing up. The cell in front is a wall, and the cell to my left is lava, so the only safe adjacent open cell is to my right at row 07, column E. To move toward the open corridor and eventually the goal, I should turn right first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07, column D facing up. The cell ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The open and safe path is to my left at column C, so I should turn left to face that direction and continue along the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am still at row 07, column D facing up. The cell ahead is a wall, so I cannot advance. The only safe open route in the corridor is to my right, so I should turn right to face east and then continue moving along the passage.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing up at row 07, column D, and the cell directly ahead is a wall. The open path is to my left toward the corridor at column C, so I should turn left to face west.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07, column D facing up. The square ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. To continue through the safe corridor, I should turn left to face west toward the open cells on my left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly ahead but one row up and three columns to the right, while the cell in front of me is lava, so I cannot move forward. Turning right will let me face the open safe cell to the east and progress around the lava toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing up at row 07, column D. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The open path is to my right, so I should turn right to face east and continue around the obstacle.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07, column D facing up. The cell ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. The safest useful move is to turn right and face the open cell to my east, which should let me navigate around the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
